records.ety: [Cf. AS. feolan to stick to, OHG. felhan, felahan, to hide, Icel. fela, Goth. filhan to hide, bury, Prov. E. feal to hide slyly, OE. felen.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Filched (flcht); p. pr. & vb. n. Filching.]

defs: To steal or take privily (commonly, that which is of little value); to pilfer.
quotes: Fain would they filch that little food away.
But he that filches from me my good name, Robs me of that which not enriches him, And makes me poor indeed.
